description:
You are acting as an edge-computing engineer who is preparing a
    lightweight “temperature-sensor” data-collector service for a fleet of
    IoT devices.  The goal is to stage everything inside your home
    directory without needing root privileges, so that a later “push” step
    can synchronise the exact same tree onto the devices.

    REQUIRED END-STATE
    1. A directory hierarchy rooted at
       /home/user/iot_edge/ exactly as described below.
    2. A runnable Python script that tails data from a named pipe.
    3. A supervisord configuration file that can manage the script as a
       service called edge-temp-sensor.
    4. A *deployment report* text file (strict format, see §5) that the
       automated grader can parse.

    ---------------------------------------------------------------------
    1. Directory structure that must exist when you finish
       /home/user/iot_edge/
       ├── sensor_collector/
       │   ├── sensor_collector.py          ← the Python service
       │   └── logs/                        ← directory, must exist
       └── supervisor/
           └── sensor_collector.conf       ← supervisord program stanza

    2. Python file requirements
       Path           : /home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/sensor_collector.py
       Behaviour      : reads (blocking) text lines from a named pipe located at
                        /tmp/sensor_pipe.  For every line it receives it must
                        prepend an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp and append the result
                        to /home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/logs/collector.log
       Mandatory lines (exact substring match, case-sensitive):
         a. She-bang   : #!/usr/bin/env python3
         b. pipe var   : pipe_path = "/tmp/sensor_pipe"
         c. log var    : log_path  = "/home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/logs/collector.log"

    3. supervisord configuration file requirements
       Path           : /home/user/iot_edge/supervisor/sensor_collector.conf
       The file must contain exactly one [program:…] stanza with the name
       edge-temp-sensor and must point to the Python script above.  
       Mandatory lines (exact substring match, case-sensitive):
         [program:edge-temp-sensor]
         command=/usr/bin/python3 /home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/sensor_collector.py
         autostart=true
         autorestart=true
         stdout_logfile=/home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/logs/supervisor_stdout.log
         stderr_logfile=/home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/logs/supervisor_stderr.log

    4. Ancillary requirements
       • Create the named pipe /tmp/sensor_pipe if it does not already exist
         so that the script can be launched immediately for testing.
       • The Python script must be made executable (chmod +x …).
       • No root privileges may be used at any step.

    5. deployment_report.log (MUST follow this exact 5-line KEY=VALUE format)
       Path : /home/user/iot_edge/deployment_report.log
       Content (exact keys, case-sensitive, in the order shown):
         SERVICE_NAME=edge-temp-sensor
         SCRIPT_PATH=/home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/sensor_collector.py
         SUPERVISOR_CONF=/home/user/iot_edge/supervisor/sensor_collector.conf
         LOG_DIR=/home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/logs
         STATUS=DEPLOYED

       Each key must appear exactly once, followed by a single “=” and then
       the value.  No additional whitespace is allowed at line starts or
       ends.  A terminating newline at the end of the file **is** required.

    ---------------------------------------------------------------------
    WHAT THE AGENT SHOULD DO
    • Create the entire directory tree (Section 1).
    • Write the Python script fulfilling Section 2 and make it executable.
    • Write the supervisord config fulfilling Section 3.
    • Establish the named pipe (Section 4).
    • Verify that supervisord *could* start the program (you may actually
      run “supervisord -n -c …” briefly, but it is not mandatory).
    • Produce the deployment_report.log exactly as per Section 5.

    The automated grader validates:
       - directory and file existence
       - the presence of mandatory substrings
       - correct KEY=VALUE lines in deployment_report.log
       - that sensor_collector.py is executable
       - that /tmp/sensor_pipe is a FIFO.

    HINTS (do NOT reveal full commands here):
       • mkdir -p, chmod, echo/heredoc redirection, and mkfifo will be your
         friends.
       • Be mindful of absolute paths; relative paths will not pass tests.
truth:
<!-- Absolute paths that must exist -->
    /home/user/iot_edge/
    /home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/
    /home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/logs/
    /home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/sensor_collector.py
    /home/user/iot_edge/supervisor/
    /home/user/iot_edge/supervisor/sensor_collector.conf
    /home/user/iot_edge/deployment_report.log
    /tmp/sensor_pipe   (must be a FIFO)

    <!-- Mandatory substrings for the Python script -->
    Shebang line must start with “#!/usr/bin/env python3”
    The file must contain: pipe_path = "/tmp/sensor_pipe"
    The file must contain: log_path  = "/home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/logs/collector.log"

    <!-- Mandatory substrings for the supervisord config -->
    [program:edge-temp-sensor]
    command=/usr/bin/python3 /home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/sensor_collector.py
    autostart=true
    autorestart=true
    stdout_logfile=/home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/logs/supervisor_stdout.log
    stderr_logfile=/home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/logs/supervisor_stderr.log

    <!-- Expected exact content of deployment_report.log -->
SERVICE_NAME=edge-temp-sensor
SCRIPT_PATH=/home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/sensor_collector.py
SUPERVISOR_CONF=/home/user/iot_edge/supervisor/sensor_collector.conf
LOG_DIR=/home/user/iot_edge/sensor_collector/logs
STATUS=DEPLOYED
    (A final newline after DEPLOYED)

    <!-- File permissions -->
    sensor_collector.py must have the executable bit set for the user.

    <!-- The tests will: -->
    1. stat(2) /tmp/sensor_pipe and ensure S_IFIFO.
    2. grep mandatory substrings in the two config files.
    3. diff deployment_report.log against the exact block above.
